fixSteps.instruction: If the door is still locked, access the manual release. On front-loaders, remove the lower front panel and locate the pull tab near the door lock. Pull to manually release.

fixSteps.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the lock repeatedly fails to unlock, the lock assembly needs replacement. The wax motor that releases the lock may be burned out.

whenToCallPro: If the manual release does not work and the door remains locked, or if the lock fails to unlock repeatedly after replacement, professional help is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag washer dU door unlock error fix

faq.q: My clothes are trapped inside. What do I do?
faq.a: First try unplugging for 10 minutes. If that fails, use the manual release tab behind the lower front panel. This manually disengages the door lock.

faq.q: Why does the door stay locked after the cycle?
faq.a: The door lock uses a wax motor element that heats up to lock and must cool to unlock. If this element fails, the door can remain locked indefinitely.

faq.q: Is this related to water still being in the tub?
faq.a: Some models will not unlock the door if water is detected in the tub. Check for drain issues if the tub is not empty when this error appears.

title: Detergent Cartridge Not Detected
shortDescription: The washer cannot detect the Load & Go detergent cartridge or dispenser unit.
fullDescription: The dET error code appears on Maytag washers equipped with the Load & Go automatic detergent dispensing system. It indicates that the washer cannot detect the detergent cartridge or bulk dispenser unit. The cartridge may not be properly seated, may be empty, or the sensor may be malfunctioning.

causes.cause: Dispenser contact pins dirty or corroded

symptoms: dET displayed on control panel
Load & Go dispenser not working
Washer may still run but does not dispense detergent
Dispenser indicator light flashing

fixSteps.title: Reseat the cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the Load & Go detergent cartridge and reinsert it firmly until it clicks into place. Make sure it is properly aligned with the rails.

fixSteps.title: Refill detergent
fixSteps.instruction: Check the detergent level in the cartridge. Refill if empty or low. The washer may not detect the cartridge if the level is too low.

fixSteps.title: Clean contact points
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the cartridge and clean the electrical contact points on both the cartridge and the washer housing. Use a cotton swab with rubbing alcohol.

fixSteps.title: Test dispenser sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning contacts does not help, the sensor may be faulty. A technician can test and replace the dispenser sensor if needed.

whenToCallPro: If the cartridge is properly seated and contacts are clean but the error persists, a technician should inspect the dispenser sensor and wiring.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag washer dET detergent cartridge error

faq.q: Can I wash without the Load & Go cartridge?
faq.a: Yes. You can add detergent directly to the main wash compartment of the dispenser drawer. The dET error will still display but will not prevent the washer from running.

faq.q: How much detergent does the Load & Go system hold?
faq.a: The Load & Go cartridge typically holds enough detergent for approximately 20-40 loads, depending on the load size and soil level selected.

faq.q: Can I use any detergent in the Load & Go system?
faq.a: Only liquid HE detergent should be used in the Load & Go system. Do not use powder detergent, pods, or non-HE detergent as these can clog the system.

title: Cycle Interrupted / Paused
shortDescription: The wash cycle has been interrupted or paused by the user.
fullDescription: The Int (Interrupted) code indicates that the wash cycle was paused or cancelled by the user pressing the Pause or Cancel button. This is an informational code, not an error. It appears when the cycle has been interrupted and is waiting for user action to continue or restart. The washer will display this code until a new cycle is started or the current cycle is resumed.

causes.cause: User pressed Pause button during cycle
Door/lid opened during cycle (if allowed by model)
Control board detected a momentary fault and paused

symptoms: Int displayed on control panel
Washer is not running but appears to be mid-cycle
Status lights indicate cycle is paused
Door may be unlocked for adding items

fixSteps.title: Resume the cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Start/Resume button to continue the wash cycle from where it was interrupted.

fixSteps.title: Cancel and restart
fixSteps.instruction: If you want to start over, press and hold the Cancel button for 3 seconds. Select a new cycle and press Start.

fixSteps.title: Check for power interruption
fixSteps.instruction: If the cycle was interrupted by a power outage, the washer may need to be restarted. Select the desired cycle and press Start.

fixSteps.title: Reset if unresponsive
fixSteps.instruction: If the washer does not respond to button presses, unplug for 5 minutes and plug back in. Select a new cycle.

whenToCallPro: The Int code does not require professional service. It is simply an informational status indicator.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag washer Int interrupted code

faq.q: Is Int an error code?
faq.a: No. Int is an informational code indicating the cycle was paused or interrupted. Simply press Start to resume or select a new cycle.

faq.q: Why does my washer keep showing Int?
faq.a: If Int appears repeatedly without user intervention, check for power supply issues, loose connections, or intermittent faults that may be causing the control board to pause.

faq.q: Can I add items when Int is displayed?
faq.a: On many models, yes. When the cycle is paused and the door unlocks, you can add or remove items before pressing Start to resume.

title: Drain Needed
shortDescription: The washer needs to drain water before proceeding with the selected operation.
fullDescription: The drn (Drain) code indicates that the washer has detected water in the tub that needs to be drained before a new cycle can begin or before the door can be unlocked. This can occur after a power interruption during a cycle, a previous drain failure, or if water has entered the tub unexpectedly.

causes.cause: Previous cycle interrupted before drain completed
Power outage during cycle left water in tub
Inlet valve leaking water into tub when off

symptoms: drn displayed on control panel
Water visible or audible in the drum
New cycle will not start
Door remains locked

fixSteps.title: Run drain cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Select the Drain & Spin cycle and press Start. The washer should pump out the standing water.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: If the drain cycle does not remove the water, check the drain hose for kinks or clogs. Straighten and clear as needed.

fixSteps.title: Clean pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the pump filter access and clean out any debris. This is the most common cause of incomplete draining.

fixSteps.title: Manual drain
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump will not operate, use the emergency drain hose to gravity drain the tub, then investigate the pump issue.

whenToCallPro: If the washer cannot drain using any method and the pump appears to be non-functional, a technician should inspect the drain pump.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag washer drn drain code fix

faq.q: Why is there water in my washer when I did not run a cycle?
faq.a: A leaking inlet valve can slowly fill the tub when the washer is not in use. Turn off water supply valves when the washer is not in use as a precaution.

faq.q: Is the drn code an error?
faq.a: It is more of a status indicator than a true error. It tells you the washer detected water and needs to drain before proceeding.

faq.q: What if the water will not drain?
faq.a: Use the emergency drain hose for manual gravity draining. Then clean the pump filter and inspect the drain path before attempting another cycle.

title: Control Lock Activated
shortDescription: The control panel lock feature has been activated, preventing any button presses.
fullDescription: The Loc (Control Lock) code indicates that the control lock feature has been activated. This child safety feature disables all control panel buttons to prevent unintended cycle changes or starts. The washer is functioning normally but will not respond to any button presses until the control lock is deactivated.

causes.cause: Control lock accidentally activated by user
UI board fault keeping lock engaged

symptoms: Loc or LC displayed on control panel
No buttons respond to presses
Lock indicator light is illuminated
Washer appears to be on but will not start

fixSteps.title: Deactivate control lock
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the Lock button (or the designated button for your model) for 3-5 seconds until the lock indicator turns off and the Loc code disappears.

fixSteps.title: Check user manual for method
fixSteps.instruction: If the Lock button does not work, consult your owner's manual. Some models use a button combination such as holding two buttons simultaneously.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: If the control lock cannot be deactivated with buttons, unplug the washer for 5 minutes. Plug back in. The control lock may reset to off.

fixSteps.title: Inspect UI board
fixSteps.instruction: If the control lock cannot be deactivated by any method, the UI board button or control lock circuit may be faulty and need replacement.

whenToCallPro: Only if the control lock cannot be deactivated through normal methods, which may indicate a faulty UI board.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag washer Loc control lock deactivate

faq.q: How do I activate the control lock?
faq.a: Press and hold the Lock button for 3-5 seconds. This prevents any settings from being changed. Useful when small children may press buttons.

faq.q: Is Loc an error code?
faq.a: No. Loc is a feature indicator showing that the control panel is locked. It is not a malfunction.

faq.q: Will the control lock survive a power outage?
faq.a: On most models, the control lock setting is remembered after power loss. You may need to deactivate it again after power is restored.
